Content_Sketch: Morning routine - you make coffee, drive to work, kiss your kid goodbye. You are ordinary.
Illustration_Note: Stickman in kitchen, pouring coffee, child at table. Warm suburban house interior.

Content_Sketch: Doctor's office. The word: end-stage renal failure. The countdown starts.
Illustration_Note: Stickman sitting across from FACELESS doctor in white coat. X-ray or chart on wall. Clinical fluorescent light.

Content_Sketch: You didn't choose this. But your body chose for you.
Illustration_Note: Stickman alone, hunched, hands on knees. Background fades to dark. Medical wristband visible.

Content_Sketch: You register for the transplant waiting list. Your number is 847. The woman behind the desk doesn't look up.
Illustration_Note: Stickman at hospital desk. FACELESS clerk. Number ticket 847 visible. Long corridor behind with other waiting patients.

Content_Sketch: Every Tuesday: dialysis. Four hours in a chair while a machine cleans your blood. The clock is the loudest thing in the room.
Illustration_Note: Stickman in hospital chair, tubes connected, dialysis machine beside. Clock on wall. Other FACELESS patients in row.

Content_Sketch: Your number moves from 847 to 843. Four spots in three months. That means four people ahead of you died.
Illustration_Note: POV of stickman hand holding updated number slip showing 843. Waiting room in background.

Content_Sketch: Your body changes. Swollen ankles. Constant fatigue. Your daughter asks why you sleep so much.
Illustration_Note: Stickman on couch, feet swollen, child stickman tugging at sleeve. Living room with family photos on wall.

Content_Sketch: Your family watches you shrink. They smile when you look. But you see their faces when they think you can't.
Illustration_Note: Stickman in doorway, family group (wife + child) in background with worried postures. Split composition: light on family, shadow on subject.

Content_Sketch: Someone at the dialysis center mentions a name. A contact. Someone who can help - outside the system.
Illustration_Note: Two stickmen in dialysis chairs. One leaning toward SUBJECT, whispering. Hospital setting, other patients visible.

Content_Sketch: Hotel lobby. A man in a dark jacket sits across from you. He speaks softly. He says: you don't need to know the details. Just be ready.
Illustration_Note: Stickman and FACELESS broker in hotel lobby. Two chairs, small table. Broker has disposable phone on table. Neutral, anonymous space.

Content_Sketch: You say no. You say you will wait. You say the system works.
Illustration_Note: Stickman standing, shaking head. Broker's business card on table between them. Stickman turning away.

Content_Sketch: Three weeks later: emergency room. Your kidneys are failing. The doctor says six months - maybe less.
Illustration_Note: Stickman in hospital bed, IV drip, monitor showing declining vitals. FACELESS doctor at bedside with clipboard.

Content_Sketch: You lie in bed staring at the ceiling. Your daughter's birthday is in seven months.
Illustration_Note: Stickman in hospital bed, eyes open, staring up. Child's drawing taped to bedside table. Dark room, single light source.

Content_Sketch: You call the number. The broker answers on the second ring. He says: I knew you would call back.
Illustration_Note: Stickman sitting on bed edge, phone to ear, hunched forward. Dim room. Medical wristband visible on phone-holding hand.

Content_Sketch: He asks one question: can you travel? You say yes. He says: don't ask anything else. You don't.
Illustration_Note: POV stickman hand holding phone. Broker's voice implied. Airline ticket materializing on bedside table.

Content_Sketch: A foreign city. A clinic with no sign on the door. The hallway smells of bleach. Staff move fast and don't make eye contact.
Illustration_Note: Stickman with overnight bag entering unmarked clinic. White hallway, FACELESS staff in scrubs passing quickly. Fluorescent light, sterile but wrong.

Content_Sketch: You lie on the operating table. You hear machines in the next room. You don't know what is happening there. You chose not to know.
Illustration_Note: POV from operating table looking up at ceiling. Surgical lights above. Sounds implied from adjacent room. Stickman hands visible at bottom of frame, medical wristband.

Content_Sketch: You wake up. There is something inside you that was not there before. It belonged to someone. You will never know who.
Illustration_Note: Stickman in recovery bed, hand on abdomen, bandage visible. Empty room, single window with light. Alone.

Content_Sketch: A nurse hands you a form. False medical declaration: voluntary donation from a family member. You sign it without reading.
Illustration_Note: Stickman in bed signing document. FACELESS nurse standing beside. Pen in hand, document on clipboard. Close framing.

Content_Sketch: Home. Your family hugs you. Your daughter says you look better. You smile. Your eyes don't.
Illustration_Note: Stickman in doorway of home, family group (wife + child) embracing. Warm light inside. Subject's face: surface smile, hollow eyes.

Content_Sketch: Every morning: one pill. Immunosuppressant. Your body will reject the kidney without it. Every pill is a reminder of what you did.
Illustration_Note: Stickman at bathroom mirror, pill bottle in hand, one pill between fingers. Reflection in mirror. Morning light.

Content_Sketch: A friend asks: so the transplant went through? A family donor? You answer the rehearsed story. It sounds almost real now.
Illustration_Note: Two stickmen at dinner table. SUBJECT speaking with calm posture. FACELESS friend across table. Normal social setting, wine glasses.

Content_Sketch: A news article: organ trafficking ring dismantled. You turn the page. But your hands are shaking.
Illustration_Note: Stickman holding newspaper/tablet, knuckles tight. Headline partially visible. Coffee cup untouched beside.

Content_Sketch: The phone rings. A number you recognize. The network. They ask: need anything else? Liver? Corneas? You hang up. You don't delete the number.
Illustration_Note: Stickman holding phone, looking at screen with unknown number. Living room setting. Finger hovering between answer and decline.

Content_Sketch: Morning. Coffee. Your daughter at the table. The same kitchen. The same life. But you carry something now that no one can see.
Illustration_Note: Mirror of BEAT_01_01: same kitchen, same routine. But stickman has pill bottle next to coffee cup. Same composition, different weight.

Content_Sketch: If you've ever wondered what it feels like to live with a part of someone else's body inside you - and never ask how it got there - now you know.
Illustration_Note: Dark background. White text. Stickman silhouette with pill bottle. Final frame.
